properties.name: Great Wall of China
properties.description: The longest structure ever built. Built over many dynasties to protect against northern invasions. You cannot actually see it from space — that's a myth.

properties.name: Christ the Redeemer
properties.description: The iconic Art Deco statue atop Corcovado mountain. Its arms stretch 28 metres wide. Lightning strikes it about 6 times per year.

properties.name: Machu Picchu
properties.description: The Inca citadel lost in the clouds. The Spanish conquistadors never found it. It was brought to international attention by Hiram Bingham in 1911.

properties.name: Chichen Itza
properties.description: The Mayan pyramid El Castillo has 365 steps — one per day of the year. At the spring equinox a serpent of light descends the staircase.

properties.name: Roman Colosseum
properties.description: The largest amphitheatre ever built. It once hosted gladiatorial contests, animal hunts, and mock naval battles. It could be flooded.

properties.name: Taj Mahal
properties.description: Emperor Shah Jahan built this white marble mausoleum for his beloved wife Mumtaz. The entire complex took 22 years and 20,000 workers to complete.

properties.name: Petra
properties.description: The 'Rose City' carved directly into rose-red sandstone cliffs by the Nabataeans. It was unknown to the Western world until 1812.

properties.name: Great Pyramid of Giza
properties.description: The only surviving wonder of the ancient world. Built as a tomb for Pharaoh Khufu, it was the tallest structure on Earth for over 3,800 years.

properties.name: Angkor Wat
properties.description: The world's largest religious monument — originally Hindu, later converted to Buddhism. It appears on Cambodia's national flag.

properties.name: Eiffel Tower
properties.description: Designed by Gustave Eiffel for the 1889 World Fair, the French public initially hated it. It is now the most visited paid monument on Earth.

properties.name: Acropolis of Athens
properties.description: The Parthenon sat undamaged for 2,000 years until a Venetian shell exploded stored gunpowder inside it in 1687. Restoration work continues today.

properties.name: Sagrada Família
properties.description: Antoni Gaudí's unfinished masterpiece — under construction for over 140 years. The main building finally received a construction permit in 2019.

properties.name: Hagia Sophia
properties.description: For nearly 1,000 years it was the world's largest cathedral. It has been a church, mosque, museum, and mosque again. Its dome inspired the design of St. Peter's Basilica.

properties.name: Stonehenge
properties.description: A prehistoric monument whose purpose remains debated. The largest stones weigh 25 tonnes and were transported 240 km from Wales — how is still a mystery.

properties.name: Mont Saint-Michel
properties.description: A tidal island topped by a medieval abbey. At high tide it becomes completely surrounded by sea. It was used as a prison during the French Revolution.

properties.name: Alhambra
properties.description: A Moorish palace complex with intricate Islamic geometry carved in plaster. Washington Irving wrote Tales of the Alhambra while living inside it in 1829.

properties.name: Easter Island Moai
properties.description: 900 giant stone statues carved by the Rapa Nui people. The famous heads have full bodies buried underground. How they were moved remains hotly debated.

properties.name: Borobudur
properties.description: The world's largest Buddhist temple, abandoned and covered by jungle for centuries. Its rediscovery was aided by Stamford Raffles in 1814.

properties.name: Sydney Opera House
properties.description: Designed by Danish architect Jørn Utzon, who won a competition at 38. He resigned midway through construction and never saw it completed.

properties.name: Statue of Liberty
properties.description: A gift from France to the United States. The copper statue has turned green due to oxidation. Her torch was closed to visitors after a bombing in 1916.

properties.name: Neuschwanstein Castle
properties.description: The fairytale castle that inspired Disney's Sleeping Beauty castle. Built by the eccentric King Ludwig II, it was opened to the public just 7 weeks after his death.

properties.name: Tikal
properties.description: One of the most powerful Mayan city-states, with temples towering above the jungle canopy. Temple IV was used as the Rebel base in Star Wars: A New Hope.

properties.name: Persepolis
properties.description: The ceremonial capital of the Achaemenid Empire. Alexander the Great burned it to the ground in 330 BC — historians still debate whether it was deliberate or an accident.

properties.name: Pompeii
properties.description: Buried by Vesuvius in 79 AD, Pompeii was perfectly preserved under ash. Plaster casts of the victims reveal their final moments in haunting detail.
